scores.reasoning: A simple yet intuitive method for improving training dynamics by assigning layer-wise learning rates based on initial gradient magnitudes.
This paper introduces a novel hierarchical network that models the body's structure as a parse graph, effectively capturing context and improving pose estimation accuracy.
This work presents a surprising and highly novel finding that pre-trained single-domain diffusion models can generate out-of-distribution samples without any retraining.
The paper introduces a novel and broadly applicable differentiable Monte Carlo variant for discontinuous integrands, enabling gradient-based optimization in challenging domains.
The proposed PMP method provides an intuitive and effective message passing paradigm for graph fraud detection by explicitly distinguishing between neighbor types.
This paper presents a novel and efficient adversarial attack where the LLM is cleverly prompted to generate its own adversarial examples.
The proposed IPRM module offers a flexible and powerful mechanism for visual reasoning by unifying iterative and parallel computation.
This work provides a clear and valuable empirical finding that pre-training on contaminated speech significantly improves downstream speech separation performance.
Mirage introduces a highly novel and efficient model-agnostic graph distillation technique by compressing the dataset's underlying computation tree distribution.
The paper makes a surprising and impactful claim that simple fine-tuning is a highly effective defense against backdoor attacks, potentially simplifying current mitigation strategies.
A practical and well-validated technique for improving training stability, though it explores a very classic optimization problem.
The proposed parse graph structure is an interesting way to inject prior knowledge, but the overall approach feels incremental in a well-established domain.
This work presents a highly novel and timely method for OOD synthesis by cleverly repurposing frozen diffusion models, challenging current scaling-focused paradigms.
A fundamental contribution that enables gradient-based optimization for a broad new class of problems involving discontinuous integrals.
An intuitive and effective message passing scheme that directly addresses the critical challenge of heterophily in graph fraud detection.
A highly novel and timely investigation into LLM robustness, proposing a clever prompt-based attack that causes a model to generate its own adversarial examples.
The proposed neuro-symbolic reasoning mechanism demonstrates strong performance on complex VQA tasks, aligning well with the current research trend towards more structured reasoning.
A useful empirical study for the speech separation community, but its narrow focus and incremental findings limit its broader impact.
This paper introduces a clever and practical model-agnostic graph distillation technique, addressing a significant bottleneck in training large GNNs.
A surprisingly simple and effective method for mitigating backdoor attacks that could become a standard practice, challenging more complex existing defenses.
This paper introduces a simple and principled heuristic to set layer-specific learning rates, potentially offering a practical improvement to standard training schemes.
The proposed parse graph introduces a strong and intuitive structural prior for human pose estimation, though its broader impact outside this specific task is limited.
This work provides a significant new perspective on diffusion models, demonstrating surprising out-of-domain generalization without retraining and advancing our understanding of their latent spaces.

This paper introduces a novel and theoretically grounded method for differentiable Monte Carlo estimation that could unlock gradient-based optimization in a wide range of problems with discontinuous integrands.
The proposed message passing scheme offers a conceptually clear and effective solution to the critical problem of mixed homophily in graph fraud detection.

The proposed IPRM introduces a flexible and powerful reasoning mechanism by unifying iterative and parallel computation, offering a significant architectural advance for complex reasoning tasks.

Excellent reproducibility, as the paper provides a public code repository, enabling direct verification of the proposed method and experimental results.
